phase.name: Testing & Examples
phase.exit_criteria: Mock transport, test helpers, runnable example programs, and open source scaffolding exist with go build/test passing

tasks.description: Implement testing subpackage with MockTransport (pre-loaded message sequences), test helper functions for building messages, and assertions for common patterns
tasks.commit: test(testing): add mock transport and test helpers
steps: Step 1: Create testing/ package (exported as agenttest to avoid conflict) with MockTransport struct
Step 2: Implement NewMockTransport(messages ...Message) that replays messages in sequence
Step 3: Add message builder helpers: NewTextMessage(), NewToolUseMessage(), NewResultMessage()
Step 4: Add assertion helpers for common test patterns
acceptance_criteria: MockTransport implements Transport interface and replays configured messages
Message builders produce valid typed messages with minimal boilerplate
Package compiles as agenttest (or similar) to avoid testing package conflict
tasks.implementation_notes: Created agenttest package with MockTransport (implements agent.Transporter, replays pre-loaded message sequences via wire-format JSON), message builders (NewTextMessage, NewToolUseMessage, NewResultMessage), and assertion helpers (AssertTextContent, AssertToolUse, AssertResult, MustNewMockTransport). Commit: ad90dc0

tasks.description: Create runnable example programs demonstrating: basic query, multi-turn client, custom hooks, MCP tools, and session management
steps: Step 1: Create examples/basic-query/ with minimal Query() usage
Step 2: Create examples/multi-turn/ demonstrating Client with follow-up queries
Step 3: Create examples/custom-tools/ showing Tool interface implementation and MCP server
acceptance_criteria: All examples compile with go build ./examples/...
Each example demonstrates a distinct SDK capability
Examples include comments explaining the pattern being demonstrated
tasks.implementation_notes: Created three examples: examples/basic-query (single-turn Query with message streaming), examples/multi-turn (Client session resumption across prompts), examples/custom-tools (in-process MCP Tool implementation via mcp.NewMCPServer). Also updated go.mod to 1.23 (required for iter.Seq2). All compile with go build ./examples/... and go test ./... passes. Commit: d548fe7

tasks.description: Prepare repository for public consumption: LICENSE (MIT), CONTRIBUTING.md, CI via GitHub Actions (build, test, vet), README with badges/install/quickstart, and tag v0.0.1 as initial release
tasks.commit: chore: add open source scaffolding (license, CI, README)
steps: Step 2: Add CONTRIBUTING.md with minimal guidance (fork, branch, test, PR)
Step 3: Add .github/workflows/ci.yml running go build ./..., go test ./..., and go vet ./... on push/PR for Go 1.23+
Step 4: Add README.md with module install, quickstart code snippet, CI badge, and pkg.go.dev badge
Step 5: Tag v0.0.1 after all tasks complete (semver, no pre-release suffix)
acceptance_criteria: go vet ./... passes with zero issues
GitHub Actions workflow is valid YAML and covers Go 1.23+
git tag v0.0.1 exists and go module is fetchable at that version
tasks.implementation_notes: Added MIT LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, .github/workflows/ci.yml (Go 1.23+ matrix, build/test/vet), and README.md with badges, install, quickstart, and examples table. Tagged v0.0.1. go vet ./... passes clean. Commit: 445d33f
